name: Glycerol, 99%+ (anhydrous)

sds_facts.storage: Store at room temperature, tightly sealed to prevent moisture uptake.

bench_knowledge: High-viscosity non-protein liquids (e.g., pure glycerol, PEG solutions) resist aspiration, causing back-pressure that stalls the piston and under-delivers volume. Wide-bore tips reduce hydraulic resistance. Reverse pipetting removes the dead-air step. A 2–3 s post-aspirate pause lets the meniscus stabilise before withdrawal.

Liquids with freeze points at or above 15°C (DMSO ~19°C, glycerol ~18°C) can solidify partially or fully in a 4°C cold room or on ice. Pipetting a partially-solidified liquid causes systematic volume errors. The physical_state guard prevents the rule from misfiring on solids whose melting point happens to be reported in freeze_point_C (e.g. NaOH pellets, PFA powder).
